cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out how long the Montgomery Bus Boycott lasted based on the information provided in the text. The boycott started after Rosa Parks was arrested on December 1, 1955. The community decided to boycott the buses on the day Parks went to court, which I assume was shortly after her arrest, maybe a few days later. However, the text doesn't specify the exact date of the court appearance, but the boycott was successful on that day. Then the community decided to continue the boycott. The boycott continued until the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that Montgomery's bus laws were unfair, which gave the African American community equal rights. The boycott ended on December 21, 1956.
